nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef slopes and lagoons; typically found on rock, rubble, and lower reef zones with lower light and moderate nutrient levels

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Benefits from supplemental feeding of small meaty foods such as brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, or nanoplankton approximately once per week. Spot feeding by pipette can improve growth and coloration. Overfeeding can degrade water quality.

goodWith: other mushroom corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals placed in direct contact (torch, hammer, frogspawn, chalice)
large urchins that may dislodge frags

summary: Hairy Mushroom Coral (Rhodactis indosinensis) is a beginner-friendly corallimorpharian distinguished by its densely furred disc. Generally tolerates a wide range of reef conditions; requires low flow and low-to-moderate lighting.
careNotes:
Rhodactis indosinensis is distinguished from other mushroom corals by its densely hairy (furry) disc surface. It is a corallimorpharian — related to stony corals but lacking a calcium carbonate skeleton. Despite being labelled a 'coral' in the hobby, it belongs to Order Corallimorpharia (Family Discosomidae).

Lighting: Low to moderate PAR is generally advised (approximately 40–150 PAR). Multiple sources suggest keeping PAR around 75–100 as a sweet spot. If polyps remain shrunken or pale in the first week, PAR may be too high — move the specimen lower. Acclimate new specimens gradually.

Flow: Low flow is strongly preferred. Direct powerhead current has been known to cause tissue stress, shrinking, and detachment. Indirect or alternating low flow is ideal.

Placement: Lower tank zones are recommended, where light and flow are gentler and conditions are more stable.

Aggression: Semi-aggressive — has been known to sting and overgrow neighboring corals if given direct contact. Maintain spacing from LPS and other corals. In large colonies, allelopathic chemical release can affect nearby corals; running activated carbon is sometimes recommended in high-density mushroom tanks.

Toxicity/Handling: Handle with care; all corallimorpharians possess nematocysts. Standard reef-keeping precautions apply (gloves recommended, wash hands thoroughly after contact). Some sources note potential palytoxin-class hazards as a general cnidarian handling precaution.

Predation capability: Rhodactis indosinensis can engulf small invertebrates and food items by expanding and closing its disc — a notable behavior compared to gentler Discosoma mushrooms. Generally does not pose a threat to passing fish or most invertebrates under normal conditions.

Reproduction: Reproduces asexually via longitudinal fission (splitting) and pedal laceration. Propagation by cutting is practiced in the hobby.

Host behavior: Clownfish have been documented using large Hairy Mushroom colonies as host anemone substitutes.

Calcium/Alkalinity demands are lower than stony corals since no skeletal deposition occurs; stable parameters matter more than precision highs.

Temperature source spread: AquariumDomain and TankFacts list 72–78°F (22–26°C); ReefChasers lists 77–84°F (25–29°C). The averaged conservative range of 23–27°C is used; the higher end (28–29°C) may be tolerated but is not well-supported across sources.
breedingNotes: Reproduces asexually in captivity via longitudinal fission and pedal laceration. Fragging by cutting mature specimens in half is a commonly practiced propagation method. Sexual reproduction occurs on natural reefs but is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: ReefChasers – Hairy Mushroom Coral Care Guide
primary.notes: Primary care guide for Rhodactis indosinensis specifically. Provided temperature, pH, alkalinity, calcium, PAR range (75–150), flow (low-moderate), care level (beginner), and adult size.

additional.name: AquariumDomain – Hairy Mushroom Coral Species Profile
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters: temperature 72–78°F (22–26°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12. Flow: moderate with alternating/indirect current preference. Confirmed semi-aggressive temperament and Indo-Pacific origin. Family listed as Discosomatidae.

additional.name: Extreme Corals – Rhodactis Mushroom Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters: temperature 76–80°F, salinity 1.024–1.026, pH 8.0–8.4, alkalinity 7–10 dKH. PAR 75–150. Confirmed beginner-to-intermediate care level and supplemental feeding 1–2x/week.

additional.name: TankFacts – Hairy Mushroom Rhodactis
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters: temperature 72–78°F (22–26°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.023–1.025, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1200–1350 ppm. Confirmed Family Discosomidae and Indo-Pacific native range.

additional.name: World Register of Marine Species (WoRMS) – Rhodactis indosinensis
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy only (AphiaID 290981). Confirmed Order Corallimorpharia, Family Discosomidae, original description by Carlgren 1943. Documented type specimens from Vietnamese and Japanese EEZs.
